title: Fragaria nigra
intro: Fragaria nigra, also known as the blackberry or black alpine strawberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been linked to reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Fragaria nigra include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fragaria nigra is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Fragaria nigra is classified as a species of the genus Fragaria, which belongs to the family Rosaceae. This classification places it within the order Rosales.
